startup.description: The Gotenberg health endpoint responds on the local listener after Chromium and LibreOffice are ready.

liveness.description: The Gotenberg health endpoint responds on the local listener.

readiness.description: A minimal HTML document renders to a PDF through the Chromium conversion route.
argv:
set -euo pipefail; html=$(mktemp --suffix=.html); pdf=$(mktemp); trap 'rm -f "$html" "$pdf"' EXIT; printf '

LV3 Gotenberg readiness

' >"$html"; curl -fsS -o "$pdf" -F "files=@${html};filename=index.html;type=text/html" http://127.0.0.1:3007/forms/chromium/convert/html; head -c 4 "$pdf" | grep -q '%PDF'

uptime_kuma.reason: The Gotenberg runtime stays private to docker-runtime-lv3 and is verified through repo-managed readiness probes plus authenticated API gateway checks.

liveness.description: The private mail gateway health endpoint responds.

readiness.description: The mail gateway can list the managed notification profiles over the authenticated API.
argv: from pathlib import Path; import sys, urllib.request; api_key = Path('/etc/lv3/mail-platform/gateway-api-key').read_text(encoding='utf-8').strip(); request = urllib.request.Request('http://127.0.0.1:8081/v1/profiles', headers={'X-API-Key': api_key}); response = urllib.request.urlopen(request, timeout=60); sys.exit(0 if response.status == 200 else 1)

monitor.name: Mail Platform Gateway Private
monitor.description: Private mail gateway health endpoint on docker-runtime-lv3.

liveness.description: The Mailpit info endpoint responds on the private HTTP listener.

readiness.description: Mailpit accepts a local SMTP probe and exposes the captured message through the REST API.
argv: import json, smtplib, urllib.request; from email.message import EmailMessage; subject = 'LV3 health-probe Mailpit'; delete_request = urllib.request.Request('http://127.0.0.1:8025/api/v1/messages', method='DELETE'); urllib.request.urlopen(delete_request, timeout=30).read(); message = EmailMessage(); message['From'] = 'healthcheck@lv3.org'; message['To'] = 'mailpit-health@lv3.org'; message['Subject'] = subject; message.set_content('Mailpit readiness probe'); client = smtplib.SMTP('127.0.0.1', 1025, timeout=30); client.send_message(message); client.quit(); response = urllib.request.urlopen('http://127.0.0.1:8025/api/v1/messages', timeout=30); payload = json.loads(response.read().decode('utf-8')); messages = payload.get('messages', []); raise SystemExit(0 if any(item.get('Subject') == subject for item in messages) else 1)

uptime_kuma.reason: Mailpit is intentionally private-only and is verified through role-level HTTP and SMTP probes instead of an always-on public monitor.

startup.description: Apache Tika startup completes once the local version endpoint returns 200 on the runtime listener.

liveness.description: Apache Tika keeps serving the local version endpoint on docker-runtime-lv3.

readiness.description: A minimal HTML document is accepted on the local `/tika` endpoint and returns clean plaintext.
argv:
set -euo pipefail; body=$(mktemp); trap 'rm -f "$body"' EXIT; printf '

LV3 Tika readiness

Readiness probe.

' | curl -fsS -X PUT -H 'Content-Type: text/html' -H 'Accept: text/plain' --data-binary @- http://127.0.0.1:9998/tika >"$body"; grep -q 'LV3 Tika readiness' "$body"

monitor.name: Tika Private
monitor.description: Private Apache Tika version endpoint on docker-runtime-lv3.

startup.description: The Tesseract OCR health endpoint responds on the local listener after the repo-built runtime starts.

liveness.description: The Tesseract OCR health endpoint responds on the local listener.

readiness.description: Tesseract OCR extracts deterministic text from the repo-managed verification image fixture.
argv:
import base64, json, urllib.request, uuid; boundary='lv3-boundary-' + uuid.uuid4().hex; image=base64.b64decode(''); chunks=[f'--{boundary}
'.encode(), b'Content-Disposition: form-data; name="file"; filename="ocr-ok.png"
', b'Content-Type: image/png

', image, b'
', f'--{boundary}--
'.encode()]; request=urllib.request.Request('http://127.0.0.1:3008/ocr', data=b''.join(chunks), method='POST', headers={'Content-Type': f'multipart/form-data; boundary={boundary}', 'Accept': 'application/json'}); payload=json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(request, timeout=60).read().decode('utf-8')); text=str(payload.get('text', '')).upper(); raise SystemExit(0 if 'OCR' in text and 'OK' in text and payload.get('page_count') == 1 else 1)

uptime_kuma.reason: The Tesseract OCR runtime remains private to docker-runtime-lv3 and is verified through repo-managed readiness probes and helper-path checks.

startup.description: Changedetection startup completes once the private root page responds on the local listener.

liveness.description: Changedetection keeps serving the private root page on docker-runtime-lv3.

readiness.description: Changedetection accepts the repo-managed API token and reports the loaded watch catalogue counts through systeminfo.
argv: set -euo pipefail; token=$(cat /etc/lv3/changedetection/api-token); systeminfo=$(mktemp); tags=$(mktemp); trap 'rm -f "${systeminfo}" "${tags}"' EXIT; curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/v1/systeminfo -H "x-api-key: ${token}" > "${systeminfo}"; curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/v1/tags -H "x-api-key: ${token}" > "${tags}"; python3 -c 'import json, pathlib, sys; payload=json.loads(pathlib.Path(sys.argv[1]).read_text()); tags=json.loads(pathlib.Path(sys.argv[2]).read_text()); assert payload["watch_count"] >= 1 and len(tags) >= 1' "${systeminfo}" "${tags}"

uptime_kuma.reason: Changedetection stays private and uses an authenticated API contract; repo-managed runtime verification plus the governed API gateway check are the supported health path.
